data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/6/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Fall on Escalating Iran Conflict, Surging Oil Prices
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded lower early Friday amid U.S.-Iran tensions and rising oil prices after an earlier drop that boosted sentiment in international stock markets. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.39%, 0.29%, and 0.21%, respectively, at 5:35 a.m. EST on March 6.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time of writing, benchmark Brent crude futures were up 1.9% to $86.93 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate crude futures were 3.6% higher at $83.93 a barrel. The U.S.-Iran war has entered its seventh day and continues to disrupt global fuel supplies. 

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major averages ended in the red due to geopolitical concerns. The S&P 500, the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite, and the Dow Jones fell 0.6%, 1.6%, and 0.3%, respectively. Overall, the Dow Jones is heading towards its second consecutive week of losses.

Coming to economic releases, investors await the release of February's nonfarm payrolls report today. The market consensus indicates a notable slowdown to about 50,000 job additions, compared with 130,000 in January 2026.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.167%. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price traded around $5,087 per ounce on Thursday.

Turning to trending stocks, Marvell Technology (MRVL) shares rallied about 12% in pre-market trading after the semiconductor company reported solid results and outlook, driven by AI-led demand. In contrast, Gap (GPS) stock was down more than 7% after the apparel company missed Q4 earnings expectations.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today as investors digested updates on the Middle East crisis and its impact on oil prices.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets rose on March 6, despite ongoing tensions in the Middle East. Investors continue to assess the impact of the U.S.-Iran conflict on energy prices.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rose 1.72%. In China, the Shanghai Composite gained 0.38%, while the Shenzhen Component increased 0.59%. 

Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei gained 0.62%, with the Topix up 0.39%.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Li Auto Stock (LI) Climbs as it Joins EV Rivals in Humanoid Robotics Club
data.description:
Shares in Chinese automaker Li Auto (LI) were in full gear today on reports that it is joining its EV rivals in the drive to develop humanoid robots.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

New Robotics Team

According to reports in Chinese media, Li Auto has been developing robotics products for nearly a year now under an internal team code-named Nexus. The unit is said to be led by He Junpei, a former hardware partner at robotics startup Jiuguang.

It is understood to be preparing to unveil a two-wheeled robot designed for work in manufacturing factories later this year. It is also said to be working on a bipedal robot under the same initiative.

Bipedals, also known as humanoids, aim to replicate human movement – walking, running, and lifting. They are expected to be used in factory settings, but also across other sectors from home care to hospitality and even surgery.

A number of other EV makers are already working on humanoid robots. These include fellow Chinese group XPeng (XPEV), which announced last year it was developing the next-generation IRON humanoid robot, featuring a bionic "bone–muscle–skin" structure with a flexible spine, synthetic muscles, soft full-body skin, and 22 degrees of freedom in its dexterous hands.

The company plans to deploy the IRON robot in commercial settings first and aims for mass production by late 2026.

EV Makers Making Moves

Another EV maker known for its humanoid production is Elon Musk's Tesla (TSLA), which is developing the Optimus humanoid. It plans to unveil the Gen 3 version of Optimus in Q1, which will include major upgrades from version 2.5, including its latest hand design. The Gen 3 is its first design meant for mass production.

EV makers are in the driving seat behind the development of humanoids for a number of reasons. The first is that they are looking to diversify their business models as EV demand stutters and competition increases.

The second is that much of the science and engineering know-how, as well as AI chips, sensors and autonomous navigation, involved in EVs are needed to power a humanoid.

China is the global leader in humanoid robotics. According to recent data from Omdia, Chinese humanoid robot makers dominated the market in 2025, with five firms in the top 10.

The global humanoid robotics market was estimated at $425 million in 2025 and is projected to reach $4.75 billion by 2032.

What are the Best Robotics Stocks to Buy Now?

We have rounded up the best robotics stocks to buy now using our TipRanks comparison tool. As can be seen below, Serve Robotics (SERV) has the biggest upside, according to analysts.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: ServiceNow Stock (NOW) Gains on CEO Comments, New Product Launches
data.description:
ServiceNow (NOW) stock rose about 6% on Thursday after the enterprise workflow automation company announced new artificial intelligence (AI)-powered products for the public sector. Moreover, investors cheered CEO William McDermott's comments that indicated that AI is not a threat but a positive for software companies. Shares of software companies like ServiceNow, Salesforce (CRM), and Microsoft (MSFT) have been under pressure recently due to concerns about potential AI disruption. NOW stock was up more than 1% in Friday's pre-market trading, along with other software stocks, amid easing AI fears.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

ServiceNow Investors Cheer CEO Comments, New Launches

At a Morgan Stanley investor conference held on Thursday, McDermott discussed how AI is enhancing the value of software companies.

"If you have a great system of record, AI is actually making your intrinsic value higher because the data that's in those systems is very important," said McDermott.

Meanwhile, at its annual Government Forum yesterday, ServiceNow launched new AI-powered solutions to deliver mission-critical outcomes for the public sector. Specifically, the company introduced its ServiceNow EmployeeWorks offering, which integrates Moveworks' (acquired by CRM in 2025) conversational AI and enterprise-grade search with ServiceNow Employee Center to create an AI front door for an agency workforce and its mission.

The company also launched Autonomous Workforce, AI specialists that can perform tasks with the "scope, authority, and governance" required in Government Community Cloud (GCC) and National Security Cloud (NSC) environments.

Is NOW Stock a Good Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on ServiceNow stock based on 30 Buys, two Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average NOW stock price target of $191.20 indicates 59% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Rising Today, 3/6/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were up Thursday late night as oil prices eased after surging earlier in the day amid rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and disruptions near the Strait of Hormuz. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were up 0.11%, 0.16%, and 0.11%, respectively, at 12:03 a.m. EST on March 6.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Oil prices edged lower in late trading, with West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude down about 1.25% to around $80.02, while Brent crude traded lower near $84.54. Earlier in the session, however, oil prices had surged after traffic through the Strait of Hormuz came to a halt. WTI crude settled up 8.5% at $81.01, its highest level since 2024, while Brent crude jumped nearly 5%, putting crude on track for its biggest weekly gain since March 2022.

In the regular trading session on Thursday, the Dow, the S&P 500, and the Nasdaq fell 1.6%, 0.6%, and 0.3%, respectively. The declines erased part of Wednesday's strong rebound, when the Dow snapped a three‑day losing streak.

Looking ahead, traders await the release of the Nonfarm Payrolls report tomorrow. The market consensus expects a sharp deceleration to about 58,000 to 65,000 new jobs, down from 130,000 in the previous month.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Unity Crushes AI Fears with Ad Tech Surge
data.description:
Unity Software Inc. (U) has been treated like collateral damage in the AI arms race. The stock is down over 50% since the start of the year, with the recent drop triggered by "world models" such as Google's (GOOGL) Project Genie — technology some investors fear could make traditional game engines obsolete.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That's a powerful narrative. It's also, in my view, fundamentally flawed.

The market is asking whether AI will replace Unity Software and the interactive experiences it provides as a leading real-time 3D development platform. A better question is whether AI actually expands Unity's relevance. Considering that Unity is integrating AI to enhance its interactive experiences, I maintain a bullish view on U stock.
data.author: Davit Kirakosyan

data.title: Pfizer's Weight-Loss Drug Approved in China — Up Against Generic Rivals
data.description:
Pfizer (PFE) has received regulatory approval in China for its GLP-1 treatment Xianweiying for long-term weight management in overweight and obese adults. The approval comes as generics enter the market, adding a new competitor to a rapidly growing obesity therapy sector worth billions. Notably, Xianweiying is part of the GLP-1 receptor agonist class, which is already offered in China by companies such as Novo Nordisk (NVO), Eli Lilly (LLY), and Innovent Biologics (IVBXF).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, GLP-1 drugs, or glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonists, help regulate blood sugar and appetite. They are commonly used for weight management and type 2 diabetes treatment.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Tumble as Oil Surges Above $80; Inflation Risks Return
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed in the red on Thursday as surging oil prices elevated inflation concerns.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • West Texas Intermediate (WTI) oil futures climbed to $81, registering the highest price since July 2024 as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has dropped by over 95%.
  • Initial jobless claims for the week ended February 28 were 213,000, below the consensus estimate of 215,000 and unchanged from the prior week.
  • U.S. employers announced 48,307 job cuts in February, down by 72% year-over-year, according to outplacement firm Challenger, Gray & Christmas.
  • President Trump fired Department of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, who will be replaced by Oklahoma Republican Senator Markwayne Mullin on March 31.
  • The U.S. is drafting new rules that would ai-chip-exports">require government approval for global chip exports, sending Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD) lower.
  • Oracle (ORCL) is planning on laying off thousands of employees to offset AI infrastructure costs, according to Bloomberg.
  • The Trade Desk (TTD) was the top-performing S&P 500 (SPX) stock after its CEO, Jeff Green, disclosed a massive $148 million insider buy. The company is also reportedly in talks with OpenAI to sell ads on ChatGPT.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Energy was the best-performing sector on Thursday with a 0.59% gain as disruptions continued to ravage the Strait of Hormuz, which handles 20% of global oil flows. Alongside gains in WTI, crude oil futures (CL) climbed by 7%, while Brent crude futures (BZ) rose 3%. Furthermore, the U.S.-Iran war has also affected oil production and exports, with Saudi Arabia suspending operations at its largest oil refinery following an Iranian drone attack and China ordering domestic refiners to suspend gasoline and diesel exports.

Several energy stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Apa Corporation (APA)
  • EOG Resources (EOG)
  • Devon Energy (DVN)
  • Chevron (CVX)
  • Coterra Energy (CTRA)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, materials was the worst-performing sector due to inflation risks from higher energy prices. Inflation fears have also lowered rate cut odds, which hurts commodity producers and materials companies because they are viewed as economically sensitive and cyclical. In addition, both gold (XAUUSD) and silver (XAGUSD) posted losses, pressuring miners.

Notable materials stocks trading lower include:

  • Martin Marietta Materials (MLM)
  • Sherwin-Williams Company (SHW)
  • Ecolab (ECL)
  • Vulcan Materials (VMC)
  • Linde (LIN)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: PLTR vs. BBAI: Which AI Defense Stock Wins Wall Street's Attention Post-Q4?
data.description:
Investors are closely watching the AI defense sector following recent earnings reports from Palantir (PLTR) and BigBear.ai (BBAI). Palantir smashed expectations with strong Q4 results, while BBAI posted a more mixed performance. Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, we compared the two stocks to see which AI defense company analysts favor post-earnings. Analysts now rate PLTR stock as a Moderate Buy, up from a previous Hold. In contrast, BBAI received a more cautious response after its earnings. While BBAI's Buy rating was maintained, price targets were cut due to declining revenues. Overall, Palantir's earnings eased valuation concerns, whereas BBAI remains a riskier play for investors.

data.title: XRP Price Keeps Getting Shot Down as $1.45 Becomes 'No Man's Land'
data.description:
The XRP (XRP-USD) market is under the pump again. After yet another failed attempt to push above the $1.43–$1.45 resistance zone, the price has slipped to $1.41. This area, which has seen repeated rejections, is now acting like a "no man's land" for bulls, where every attempt to reclaim higher ground is met with immediate selling pressure. To make matters worse, trading volume during this recent slide spiked 74% above average, signaling that sellers are still in firm control of the short-term direction.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sellers Maintain Control of Market Structure

The current price action is a continuation of a broader struggle that has defined XRP since its 2025 peak. While institutional interest, tracked through steady spot ETF inflows, has provided some support, it hasn't been enough to offset the persistent selling at these key psychological ceilings. This mismatch between long-term institutional buying and short-term speculative selling has trapped XRP in a tight, difficult-to-navigate corridor.

Traders Watch the Critical $1.40 Level

All eyes are now focused on the $1.40 support mark. This is the pivotal line of defense. If buyers can hold this floor, the market may stabilize, offering a chance to regroup for another attempt at breaking that $1.45 ceiling. However, if this level fails to hold, the path toward lower demand zones near $1.33, or even $1.00 in an extreme scenario, could quickly open up.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.3956.
data.author: Annika Masrani
